Module com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java
Package com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.internal

Class StringVerifierNoOp

java.lang.Object
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.internal.extension.AbstractObjectVerifierNoOp<StringVerifier,String>
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.internal.StringVerifierNoOp
All Implemented Interfaces:
ExtensibleObjectVerifier<StringVerifier,String>, StringVerifier
public final class StringVerifierNoOp extends AbstractObjectVerifierNoOp<StringVerifier,String> implements StringVerifier
A StringVerifier that does nothing.

    • asInetAddress

      public StringVerifier asInetAddress(Consumer<InetAddressVerifier> consumer)
      Description copied from interface: StringVerifier
      Verifies nested requirements. This mechanism can be used to group related requirements.

      See StringVerifier.asInetAddress() for exceptions that may be thrown to the consumer.

      Specified by:
      asInetAddress in interface StringVerifier
      Parameters:
      consumer - verifies Internet addresses
      Returns:
      the updated verifier

    • asUri

      public UriVerifier asUri()
      Description copied from interface: StringVerifier
      Returns a verifier for the URI representation of the value.

      Technically-speaking, there is no such thing as an invalid URI format. Per RFC3986, any String can be represented as a relative URI, but Java's implementation is based on an older specification where this was not the case.

      Specified by:
      asUri in interface StringVerifier
      Returns:
      a verifier for the URI representation of the value
      See Also:
      • Discussion of Java URI vs RFC3986

    • asUri

      public StringVerifier asUri(Consumer<UriVerifier> consumer)
      Description copied from interface: StringVerifier
      Verifies nested requirements. This mechanism can be used to group related requirements.

      See StringVerifier.asUri() for exceptions that may be thrown to the consumer.

      Specified by:
      asUri in interface StringVerifier
      Parameters:
      consumer - verifies URIs
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
